data.title: REGENXBIO Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Contact the DJS Law Group to Discuss Your Rights - RGNX
data.description:
REGENXBIO Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Contact the DJS Law Group to Discuss Your Rights - RGNX

REGENXBIO Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Contact the DJS Law Group to Discuss Your Rights - RGNX

PR Newswire

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 20, 2026

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 20, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The DJS Law Group  reminds investors of a class action lawsuit against  REGENXBIO Inc. ("Regenxbio " or "the Company") (NASDAQ: RGNX ) for violations of §§10(b) and 20(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and Rule 10b-5 promulgated thereunder by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shareholders who purchased shares of RGNX during the class period listed are encouraged to contact the firm regarding possible lead plaintiff appointments. Appointment as lead plaintiff is not required to partake in any recovery.

CLASS PERIOD:  February 9, 2022 to January 27, 2026

DEADLINE: April 14, 2026

CASE DETAILS: According to the Complaint, the Company made false and misleading statements to the market. Regenxbio made consistently positive statements to the market about the safety and efficacy of its RGX-111 product candidate. Meanwhile, it was concealing adverse information on safety and efficacy from investors. The FDA placed a clinical hold on RGX-111 when it was revealed a clinical trial participant had developed a tumor. Based on these facts, Regenxbio's public statements were false and materially misleading throughout the class period.

If you are a shareholder who suffered a loss, contact us to participate .

WHY DJS LAW GROUP?  DJS Law Group's primary focus is to enhance investor return through balanced counseling and aggressive advocacy. We specialize in securities class actions, corporate governance litigation, and domestic/international M&A appraisals. Our clients are some of the largest and most sophisticated hedge funds and alternative asset managers in the world. The litigation claims of our clients are extraordinarily valuable assets that demand respect, focus, and results.

Join the case to recover your losses.

This press release may be considered Attorney Advertising in some jurisdictions under the applicable law and rules of ethics.

CONTACT:
David J. Schwartz
DJS Law Group
274 White Plains Road, Suite 1
Eastchester, NY 10709
Phone: 914-206-9742
Email: David@djslawllp.com

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-inc-sued-for-securities-law-violations---contact-the-djs-law-group-to-discuss-your-rights--rgnx-302693373.html

SOURCE DJS Law Group LLP

data.title: ENPH Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Enphase Energy, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
data.description:
ENPH Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Enphase Energy, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm

ENPH Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Enphase Energy, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm

PR Newswire

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 20, 2026

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 20, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Schall Law Firm, a national shareholder rights litigation firm, reminds investors of a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. ("Enphase" or "the Company") (NASDAQ: ENPH) for violations of §§10(b) and 20(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and Rule 10b-5 promulgated thereunder by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Investors who purchased the Company's securities between April 22, 2025 and October 28, 2025, inclusive (the "Class Period"), are encouraged to contact the firm before April 20, 2026.

If you are a shareholder who suffered a loss, click here to participate.

We also encourage you to contact Brian Schall of the Schall Law Firm, 2049 Century Park East, Suite 2460, Los Angeles, CA 90067, at 310-301-3335, to discuss your rights free of charge. You can also reach us through the firm's website at www.schallfirm.com, or by email at bschall@schallfirm.com.

The class, in this case, has not yet been certified, and until certification occurs, you are not represented by an attorney. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member.

According to the Complaint, the Company made false and misleading statements to the market. Enphase misstated its capability to manage channel inventory. The Company overstated its ability to manage the negative impact of the termination of the Residential Clean Energy Credit pursuant to Internal Revenue Code Section 25D. Based on these facts, the Company's public statements were false and materially misleading throughout the class period. When the market learned the truth about Enphase, investors suffered damages.

Join the case to recover your losses

The Schall Law Firm represents investors around the world and specializes in securities class action lawsuits and shareholder rights litigation.

This press release may be considered Attorney Advertising in some jurisdictions under the applicable law and rules of ethics.

CONTACT:
The Schall Law Firm
Brian Schall, Esq.,
www.schallfirm.com
Office: 310-301-3335
info@schallfirm.com

data.title: Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) Market Forecast 2026-2036: Market to Reach USD 9.5 Billion by 2036 at 6.8% CAGR
data.description:
IBC demand is rising as reusable bulk packaging boosts logistics efficiency, cuts costs, and ensures safe, compliant handling across global industries.

NEWARK, DELAWARE / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2026 / The global Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) market is entering a phase of steady, structurally driven expansion, supported by the growing need for efficient bulk storage and transport solutions across industrial value chains. Valued at USD 4.9 billion in 2026, the market is projected to reach USD 9.5 billion by 2036, advancing at a CAGR of 6.8% during the forecast period. This growth reflects the increasing reliance on high-capacity, reusable packaging systems that optimize logistics efficiency, reduce handling costs, and ensure compliance with stringent safety regulations.

According to a comprehensive strategic outlook from Future Market Insights (FMI), the IBC market's trajectory is closely linked to industrial expansion, global trade flows, and the rising need for safe handling of liquids, powders, and granular materials across sectors such as chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and food processing.

Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) Market Snapshot (2026-2036)

Metric

Value (2026-2036)

Current Market Value (2026)

USD 4.9 Billion

Projected Market Value (2036)

USD 9.5 Billion

Global Growth Rate (CAGR)

6.8%

Leading Segment

Plastic IBCs (52.3%)

Top Growth Market

India (9.7% CAGR)

Discover Growth Opportunities in the Market - Get Your Sample Report Now: https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/sample/rep-gb-2036

The Structural Shift: From Conventional Packaging to Bulk Efficiency

The IBC market is witnessing a clear shift from traditional drums and barrels toward more advanced bulk packaging formats. IBCs combine stackability, reusability, and higher storage capacity, enabling companies to optimize warehouse space and transportation costs. This transition is particularly evident in industries dealing with hazardous or sensitive materials, where safety, compliance, and contamination control are critical.

Manufacturers are increasingly focusing on durability, modular designs, and compliance with global safety standards. Rigid and flexible IBC variants are gaining traction for their ability to streamline supply chains and minimize product loss during storage and transit.

Pricing Power and Market Consolidation

The competitive dynamics of the IBC market are shaped by high entry barriers and strong pricing power among established players. Large manufacturers leverage economies of scale, long-standing customer relationships, and advanced production capabilities to maintain profitability while offering competitive pricing.

Significant capital investments in manufacturing infrastructure and strict regulatory compliance requirements limit the entry of new players. However, the market remains moderately fragmented due to the wide range of industries served, each with unique storage and transportation needs.

As demand grows, consolidation is expected to accelerate. Larger companies are likely to acquire smaller, niche players to expand their product portfolios, improve operational efficiency, and strengthen their global footprint. This consolidation trend will play a key role in defining market leadership over the next decade.

Growth Trajectory: A Decade of Incremental Expansion

The IBC market is projected to witness consistent growth across the forecast period, with a clear progression in value:

  • 2026: USD 4.9 billion

  • 2031: USD 7.8 billion

  • 2036: USD 9.5 billion

Between 2026 and 2031, the market is expected to grow by USD 2.9 billion, driven by increasing adoption across chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and food and beverage sectors. These industries rely heavily on IBCs for their durability, efficiency, and ability to handle bulk volumes safely.

From 2031 to 2036, the market will add an additional USD 1.7 billion. While growth may moderate slightly as the market matures, demand will remain strong due to the continued expansion of global supply chains and the need for efficient bulk handling solutions.

Segment Spotlight: Material and Application Leadership

Plastic IBCs Dominate Material Demand

Plastic IBCs lead the market, capturing 52.3% of the total share. Their dominance is attributed to their lightweight nature, cost-effectiveness, and strong resistance to corrosion. These features make them suitable for a wide range of applications, including chemicals, food products, and pharmaceuticals.

Additionally, plastic IBCs support sustainability initiatives, as they can be recycled and reused, aligning with the growing emphasis on circular economy practices.

Chemical Storage Drives Application Growth

Chemical storage remains the leading application segment, accounting for 38.4% of the market. The need for safe, reliable, and compliant storage solutions for hazardous and non-hazardous chemicals is a key driver of demand.

IBCs offer superior durability, leak prevention, and ease of handling, making them ideal for transporting large volumes of chemical products. As global chemical production continues to rise, this segment is expected to maintain its dominance.

Key Drivers: Efficiency, Safety, and Sustainability

Several factors are driving the expansion of the IBC market:

  • Logistics Optimization: IBCs reduce handling time, improve storage efficiency, and lower transportation costs.

  • Industrial Growth: Expansion in chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and food processing industries is fueling demand.

  • Regulatory Compliance: Strict safety standards are pushing industries toward standardized bulk packaging solutions.

  • Sustainability Trends: Reusable and recyclable IBCs support environmental goals and reduce waste.

The integration of automation in manufacturing and logistics is further boosting demand, as IBCs are well-suited for mechanized handling systems.

Challenges: Cost and Operational Complexity

Despite strong growth prospects, the IBC market faces several challenges. The high initial investment required for IBC procurement can be a barrier for small and medium-sized enterprises. Additionally, the need for specialized handling equipment and storage infrastructure increases operational costs.

Supply chain disruptions and fluctuations in raw material prices, particularly plastics and metals, can also impact production costs. Furthermore, strict regulations regarding cleaning, sanitization, and reuse add complexity to operations, particularly in industries such as food and pharmaceuticals.

Regional Powerhouses: Asia Leads Growth

The global IBC market is witnessing strong growth across both developed and emerging regions:

  • India (9.7% CAGR): The fastest-growing market, driven by rapid industrialization, expanding manufacturing capabilities, and government initiatives supporting infrastructure development.

  • China (8.4% CAGR): A key hub for manufacturing and exports, with strong demand from chemicals and industrial sectors.

  • Brazil (7.8% CAGR): Growth supported by agriculture, food processing, and export-oriented industries.

  • Italy (6.3% CAGR): Steady demand from food, beverage, and chemical sectors.

  • United States (6.2% CAGR): Mature market driven by sustainability initiatives and advanced logistics infrastructure.

Asia Pacific remains the dominant region, supported by rapid industrial growth and increasing trade activities.

Dynamics of the Decade: Smart and Sustainable IBCs

Looking ahead to 2036, several emerging trends are expected to redefine the IBC market:

  • Eco-Friendly Materials: Growing adoption of recyclable and biodegradable materials to reduce environmental impact.

  • Smart IBCs: Integration of IoT-enabled sensors for real-time monitoring of temperature, pressure, and contents.

  • Hygienic Design: Increased focus on contamination control in pharmaceuticals and food industries.

  • Automation Compatibility: Development of IBCs optimized for automated logistics and handling systems.

These innovations are transforming IBCs from simple storage containers into intelligent, value-added components of modern supply chains.

Competitive Landscape: Innovation and Scale

The IBC market remains highly competitive, with key players focusing on innovation, customization, and sustainability to strengthen their market positions. Leading companies are leveraging their global presence and manufacturing expertise to offer comprehensive solutions across industries.

Major players in the IBC market include: Mauser Packaging Solutions, Greif Inc., Schott AG, Time Technoplast Ltd., Hoover Ferguson Group, CL Smith, Schutz Container Systems, ALPLA Group, Berry Global Inc., and Composite IBC Inc.

These companies compete by enhancing product durability, improving safety standards, and developing sustainable solutions. As demand for bulk packaging continues to rise, the focus on innovation and efficiency will intensify, shaping the future of the market.

For an in-depth analysis of evolving formulation trends and to access the complete strategic outlook for the Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) Market through 2036, visit the official report page at: https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/intermediate-bulk-container-market

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 4 AM ET: Oil Prices Head for 5% Weekly Gain on U.S.-Iran Fears | U.S. ...
data.description:
Oil Prices Head for 5% Weekly Gain on U.S.-Iran Fears

Oil prices were on track for weekly gains of more than 5%, buoyed by rising concerns of a potential U.S. attack against Iran.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Post Hefty Decline

Commercial crude oil stocks were down by 9 million barrels last week. Analysts expected crude stocks to rise by 1.1 million barrels.

----

Crude Glut at Sea Keeps Traders Guessing as Prices Hold on Geopolitical Risk

Futures remain resilient, supported by geopolitical risk premiums, despite estimates that the market ran a surplus of roughly 1.5 million barrels a day in 2025.

----

Repsol Lifts Dividend, Maintains Buyback Pace

Spanish energy company Repsol maintained the pace of its share buyback and raised its dividend for 2026.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil futures, Repsol and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

----

Rising Demand for Nuclear Power Pressures Uranium Supply

The surge in demand for data centers and nuclear power to keep them running is stressing the global uranium supply, raising concerns about a potential shortage of fuel for reactors.

----

Ukraine's Neighbors Threaten to Cut Off Energy Supplies

Halting power and diesel from Hungary and Slovakia would compound the impact of Russian attacks that have left millions of Ukrainians without power.

----

Naturgy Profit Rises on Higher Energy Demand, Pricing

Gas and electricity prices remained higher on average during the year, driven by geopolitical tensions and macroeconomic uncertainty.

----

The Fossil Fuel Tycoon Teaming Up With the Rockefellers to Fight Energy Poverty

EQT Chief Executive Toby Rice is starting a nonprofit to tackle a lack of access to modern energy infrastructure in poor nations.

----

JLL Partners Closes Ninth Fund at $1.4 Billion

JLL Partners closed its ninth flagship fund with $1.4 billion, the midmarket private-equity firm said Wednesday.

----

Quantum Capital Raises $2.3 Billion So Far for New Energy Fund and Scores an Exit

The private-equity firm is set to reap a more than twofold gross return from the $4.7 billion sale of natural-gas fired power plants backed by the new fund. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 20, 2026 04:15 ET (09:15 GMT)

data.title: Global Micro Irrigation System Market to Reach USD 33.0 Billion by 2036 as Automation, Precision Water Management, and Scalable Farm Infrastructure Redefine Agricultural Productivity
data.description:
Automation-Driven Irrigation Expansion Accelerates Precision Agriculture Across High-Growth Economies

NEWARK, DELAWARE / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2026 / The global micro irrigation system market is entering a new era of industrial-scale transformation, driven by the convergence of automation, precision agriculture, and water efficiency mandates. Valued at USD 13.1 billion in 2026, the market is projected to surge to USD 33.0 billion by 2036, expanding at a robust 9.7% CAGR, according to a comprehensive analysis by Future Market Insights (FMI). This growth reflects a structural shift in farming operations, where irrigation is no longer treated as a seasonal input but as a measurable, controllable production layer essential for yield optimization and operational efficiency.

As agricultural enterprises scale operations across dispersed acreage, irrigation infrastructure is increasingly engineered around predictable hydraulic performance, automation-ready control systems, and modular components capable of supporting multi-season reliability. This transformation positions micro irrigation systems as a foundational technology in modern agricultural productivity frameworks.

Irrigation Evolution: From Manual Utility to Automated Production Infrastructure

The industry is witnessing a decisive transition toward automation-enabled irrigation ecosystems, where execution is governed by sensor-driven telemetry, programmable controllers, and standardized hydraulic designs. This shift allows operators to maintain consistent water delivery while minimizing labor variability and operational risk.

"Farm operators are prioritizing irrigation architectures that enable repeatable execution, remote diagnostics, and scalable deployment across estates," notes an FMI analyst. "Investment decisions are increasingly tied to measurable KPIs such as water-use efficiency, yield stability, and labor optimization, reinforcing irrigation's role as a strategic production asset rather than a maintenance function."

This industrialization of irrigation systems is accelerating procurement of components designed for high reliability, integrated filtration, and interoperability with farm management software, ensuring operational continuity and lifecycle cost predictability.

Get Access of Report Sample -
https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/sample/rep-gb-4303

Procurement Transformation: Reliability, Serviceability, and Standardization Take Priority

Procurement frameworks are rapidly evolving, with buyers placing greater emphasis on component durability, installation quality, and service ecosystem maturity. Downtime is now quantified not only in repair costs but also in lost productivity, yield exposure, and labor disruptions.

Key procurement trends include:

  • Standardized component stacks to simplify maintenance and reduce commissioning complexity

  • Automation-ready controllers to enable centralized monitoring and remote calibration

  • Robust filtration systems to maintain consistent hydraulic performance under varying water quality conditions

  • Structured service models to support preventive maintenance and ensure uptime during peak irrigation cycles

This shift is fostering closer alignment between irrigation infrastructure and broader agricultural automation strategies, reinforcing irrigation as a core pillar of precision farming.

Segment Leadership: Drip Irrigation and Emitters Anchor Market Expansion

Micro irrigation systems are increasingly deployed across diverse agricultural environments, but certain segments continue to dominate due to their performance efficiency and scalability.

Drip irrigation systems lead with a commanding 77% market share, driven by their ability to deliver uniform, targeted water application while supporting scalable zoning across large land areas. These systems enable gradual upgrades in automation sophistication without requiring costly hydraulic redesigns.

On the component side, drippers and emitters account for 27% of total demand, reflecting their central role in ensuring delivery precision and system reliability. Maintenance strategies are increasingly built around emitter performance, particularly in regions with variable water quality.

Orchards and vineyards represent the largest crop segment, accounting for 41% of market share, as perennial crop systems rely heavily on consistent irrigation scheduling to maintain yield quality and long-term productivity.

Meanwhile, open-field cultivation dominates farming techniques with a 45% share, supported by large-scale mechanized agriculture and increasing integration of irrigation automation with precision farming equipment.

Regional Growth Engines: Emerging Markets Accelerate Adoption

Country

CAGR (2026-2036)

Key Growth Drivers

India

12.4%

Large-scale adoption programs, water conservation initiatives, and increasing automation across commercial farming operations. Government-backed incentives and modernization programs are accelerating deployment across diverse crop systems.

China

11.7%

Infrastructure development, scalable deployment strategies, and growing emphasis on operational efficiency across large agricultural production zones.

Japan

9.7%

Adoption of precision agriculture practices and structured operational frameworks focused on measurable efficiency gains and optimized irrigation execution.

United States

9.0%

Upgrades to automation-enabled irrigation scheduling, remote monitoring systems, and strong focus on lifecycle cost optimization and productivity enhancement.

Data-Driven Agriculture: Automation and Interoperability Define Competitive Advantage

Automation and interoperability are becoming defining factors in irrigation system selection. Buyers increasingly prioritize systems that integrate seamlessly with enterprise farm management platforms, enabling centralized monitoring, analytics-driven decision-making, and scalable performance management.

Key technological trends shaping the market include:

  • Closed-loop irrigation execution based on real-time environmental data

  • Remote monitoring and diagnostics to ensure operational continuity

  • Standardized controller interfaces to maintain interoperability across vendors

  • Predictive maintenance programs to reduce downtime and optimize lifecycle performance

These capabilities allow operators to implement repeatable irrigation strategies across multiple sites while maintaining operational consistency.

Competitive Landscape: Deployment Execution and Lifecycle Performance Drive Market Leadership

Competition in the micro irrigation system market is increasingly defined by deployment execution strength, service reliability, and system scalability, rather than mechanism variety alone. Suppliers are strengthening market positioning through standardized product architectures, improved filtration systems, and enhanced field support capabilities.

Key players actively shaping the global market include:

  • Netafim Ltd.

  • Jain Irrigation Systems Ltd.

  • The Toro Company

  • Rain Bird Corporation

  • Hunter Industries

  • Valmont Industries

  • Rivulis Irrigation Ltd.

  • Lindsay Corporation

  • Reinke Manufacturing Co., Inc.

  • EPC Industries Ltd.

These companies are investing in advanced controller integration, scalable deployment models, and service-driven differentiation to support multi-year infrastructure upgrades without disrupting operational continuity.

Future Outlook: Irrigation Automation to Anchor Sustainable Agricultural Expansion

Looking ahead to 2036, micro irrigation systems are expected to play a central role in enabling sustainable agricultural expansion, particularly as global water constraints intensify and productivity demands increase.

Key long-term trends include:

  • Expansion of automation-driven irrigation infrastructure

  • Increased adoption of telemetry and analytics-enabled irrigation systems

  • Greater emphasis on lifecycle reliability and serviceability

  • Integration with broader precision agriculture ecosystems

As agriculture transitions toward data-driven, automation-led production models, micro irrigation systems will remain critical in delivering consistent yields, optimizing resource utilization, and ensuring long-term operational resilience.

With sustained investment in automation, infrastructure modernization, and precision water management, the micro irrigation system market is poised to emerge as one of the fastest-growing segments within the global agricultural technology landscape over the next decade.

data.title: Sai Life Sciences to Recruit 700+ Professionals in FY27
data.description:
Sai Life Sciences to Recruit 700+ Professionals in FY27

Sai Life Sciences Limited (BSE: 544306 | NSE: SAILIFE), one of India’s leading integrated contract research, development and manufacturing organizations (CRDMOs), today announced plans to hire 700+ scientific, technical, and management professionals during 2026–27 as it scales capabilities to meet growing global demand for end-to-end drug discovery, development and manufacturing services.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260220711675/en/

Integrated R&D Campus, Sai Life Sciences, Hyderabad, India

The recruitment will span roles across medicinal chemistry, biology, DMPK, process and analytical development, formulation development, process engineering, technology transfer, quality, peptides, business development, program management, and manufacturing, among others.

A specific area of focus through this recruitment drive will be on attracting high-calibre scientists from leading institutions in India and globally, reflecting the increasing complexity of programs being entrusted to Sai Life Sciences and the higher scientific expectations placed on Indian CRDMOs by global innovator companies.

Making the announcement, Krishna Kanumuri, CEO & Managing Director, Sai Life Sciences, said: “We are at an inflection point for the Indian CRDMO industry. Global supply-chain rebalancing, the need for resilient development and manufacturing partners, and the rising sophistication of outsourced science are converging in India’s favour. Demand for high-quality, integrated partners is already visible, and this expansion is about preparing ourselves to serve that demand at scale — with the right infrastructure, strong digital and quality systems, and deep scientific capability.”

Krishna added, “For scientists, this is a genuinely exciting moment. They have the opportunity to work on globally relevant, high-impact programs while being based in India. We believe this phase of growth creates a compelling opportunity for Indian scientists anywhere in the world to consider building the next chapter of their careers here—without compromising on scientific rigor, exposure, or the quality of work.”

The new roles will support growing activity in areas such as complex small-molecule synthesis, high-throughput experimentation, data-enabled drug discovery, and late-stage CMC and commercial manufacturing scale-up.

As part of its talent strategy, Sai Life Sciences has launched a global alumni engagement platform and is strengthening learning, leadership development, and internal mobility programs to support long-term career growth.

Sai Life Sciences operates across India, the UK, and the US, serving global pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies. Its Manchester site focuses on process R&D, while its Boston Biology facility supports early discovery collaborations and client engagement. The majority of new roles will be based in Hyderabad, home to the company’s largest integrated R&D campus. With more than 3,400 scientists and professionals currently on board, the planned hiring reflects its continued growth and its expanding role as a global CRDMO partner.

About Sai Life Sciences

Sai Life Sciences is a full-service CRDMO working with innovator pharma and biotech companies globally to accelerate the discovery, development, and commercialization of small molecules. With operations across India, the UK, and the US, Sai Life Sciences offers scientific expertise, world-class infrastructure, and a culture of excellence focused on quality, safety, and sustainability. www.sailife.com

For further information, contact:

Sriram Gopalakrishnan
Vice President, Corporate Communications
Sai Life Sciences  
Ph: +91 9121295355
sriram.g@sailife.com

data.title: Anglo American Writes Down De Beers Diamond Unit Value by Another $2.3 Billion — Update
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker 




  Anglo American wrote down the value of its De Beers diamond business by $2.3 billion, the third cut in three years, as challenging market conditions complicate plans to dispose of the unit. 



  The London-listed miner warned earlier this month that it was undertaking a review of the business that could lead to the write-down. For 2025, the unit booked a roughly $500 million underlying earnings loss. 



  Soft economic growth in China, and the rise of cheaper lab-made diamonds have taken the shine off the storied unit. Diamond jewelry retailers are increasingly embracing synthetic diamonds, especially in products such as engagement rings. 



  In February last year, Anglo wrote down De Beers's value by $2.9 billion, having already reduced it by $1.6 billion in 2024. 



  After fending off a roughly $50 billion hostile takeover bid from BHP in 2024, the miner has been simplifying its business by spinning off units and focusing on iron and copper. It agreed to merge with Canada's Teck Resources in September in a deal that places copper at the heart of the combined entity. 



  The rise of artificial diamonds has dented sales for De Beers, whose famous 1947 "A Diamond is Forever" campaign enhanced the appeal of diamond rings. Weakness in the industrial diamond market has added to the problems. 



  The company said Friday that it was progressing with the separation of De Beers. It is prioritizing a sale of the business and given the continuing discussions with interested parties, Chief Executive Duncan Wanblad said he is optimistic a deal will be signed over 2026. 



  The impairment came as it booked underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization of $6.4 billion for last year compared with $6.3 billion in 2024. It booked a net loss of $3.7 billion, including the $2.3 billion De Beers pretax impairment. 





  Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 20, 2026 03:49 ET (08:49 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Rheinmetall to Supply Components for German Army's New Reconnaissance Vehicles
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo 




  Rheinmetall received a multimillion-euro package of orders to supply components for reconnaissance vehicles purchased by the German army. 



  The package is valued in the mid-hundreds of millions of euros, Rheinmetall said. 



  The German arms maker will supply turrets, automatic cannons and modern simulators for the Luchs 2 reconnaissance vehicle, the company said Friday. 



  The prime contractor for the Luchs 2 is General Dynamics European Land Systems, which will deliver them to the German army from 2029. 



  The majority of these components, including the turrets, will be manufactured in Bremen, while the automatic cannons will be produced in Rome, Rheinmetall said. 



  The contract was signed at the beginning of February. 





  Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 20, 2026 03:50 ET (08:50 GMT)

data.title: Fermi Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Contact the DJS Law Group to Discuss Your Rights - FRMI
data.description:
Fermi Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Contact the DJS Law Group to Discuss Your Rights - FRMI

Fermi Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Contact the DJS Law Group to Discuss Your Rights - FRMI

PR Newswire

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 20, 2026

LOS ANGELES, Feb. 20, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The DJS Law Group reminds investors of a class action lawsuit against Fermi Inc. ("Fermi " or "the Company") (NASDAQ: FRMI) for violations of the federal securities laws.

Shareholders who purchased shares of FRMI during the class period listed are encouraged to contact the firm regarding possible lead plaintiff appointments. Appointment as lead plaintiff is not required to partake in any recovery.

CLASS PERIOD:  pursuant and/or traceable to Fermi's initial public offering ("IPO") conducted in October 2025, and/or between October 1, 2025, and December 11, 2025, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period").

DEADLINE: March 6, 2026

CASE DETAILS: According to the Complaint, the Company made false and misleading statements to the market. Fermi's "Project Matador" campus was largely depending on a funding commitment from a single potential tenant who was at risk of terminating this commitment. The Company understated the extent to which it relied on this tenant to investors. Based on these facts, Fermi's public statements were false and materially misleading throughout the IPO period.

If you are a shareholder who suffered a loss, contact us to participate .

WHY DJS LAW GROUP?  DJS Law Group's primary focus is to enhance investor return through balanced counseling and aggressive advocacy. We specialize in securities class actions, corporate governance litigation, and domestic/international M&A appraisals. Our clients are some of the largest and most sophisticated hedge funds and alternative asset managers in the world. The litigation claims of our clients are extraordinarily valuable assets that demand respect, focus, and results.

Join the case to recover your losses.

This press release may be considered Attorney Advertising in some jurisdictions under the applicable law and rules of ethics.

CONTACT:
David J. Schwartz
DJS Law Group
274 White Plains Road, Suite 1
Eastchester, NY 10709
Phone: 914-206-9742
Email: David@djslawllp.com

SOURCE DJS Law Group LLP

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 3 AM ET: Amazon Is Now America's Biggest Company. Its 17-Year Journey to Surpass Walmart. | Private-Credit ...
data.description:
Amazon Is Now America's Biggest Company. Its 17-Year Journey to Surpass Walmart.

Walmart had held the title as the biggest company by annual revenue since 2009.

----

Private-Credit Warning Signs Flash After Blue Owl Unloads $1.4 Billion in Assets

The sale raises fears that the industry's efforts to court individual investors will suffer.

----

Anglo American Writes Down De Beers Diamond Unit Value by Another $2.3 Billion

It is the third time in three years that Anglo American writes down the value of its diamond business.

----

Air Liquide Posts Higher Profit on Sales Growth in Gas, Services Business

Air Liquide posted a rise in full-year net profit as all gas and services businesses-accounting for most of group sales-recorded 2% sales growth.

----

Perseus Mining Focused on Internal Growth; Will Keep Predictive Stake For Now

Perseus Mining's chief executive said the gold producer is focused on developing its own growth options after a failed bid for Predictive Discovery and has no current plans to sell its stake in the Africa-focused explorer.

----

Newmont CEO Prioritizes Margins, Not 'Starry-Eyed' About Record Gold Prices

Newmont will focus on widening margins as it capitalizes on a record rally in gold prices, its chief executive said, after the world's largest gold miner reported a drop in fourth-quarter profit on higher royalties and taxes.

----

Theft of Trade Secrets Is on the Rise-and AI Is Making It Worse

Google, Apple and xAI are among companies that have sought to defend their sensitive information as employees are accused of stealing it.

----

West Virginia Attorney General's Lawsuit Alleges Apple's iCloud Allows for Distribution of Child Pornography

The lawsuit said the platform made it easier to maintain and repeatedly access large collections of illicit material.

----

Live Nation Reports Growing Annual Concert Attendance as Antitrust Trial Looms

The live entertainment ticket provider faces Justice Department allegations that it illegally monopolized markets for concert booking and ticketing.

----

NASA Boss Blasts Starliner Mission That Left Astronauts in Space for Months

Officials prioritized agency goals over crew safety, the administrator said. A NASA report found fault with Boeing and the space agency.

----

Nation-State Hackers Hit Businesses for Commercial Edge

With all eyes on safeguarding U.S. infrastructure, the nation's top cyber adversaries are increasingly targeting companies to pinch trade secrets, aiming to give their own industries a competitive edge in global markets.

----

From Kids to Collectors: Toys Pivot Towards Older Fans

Toymakers are increasingly targeting teens and adults and racing to turn streaming hits and established franchises into high-end collectibles.

----

Deere Boosts Outlook, Sees Construction, Small Agriculture Demand Recovering

Deere posted lower first-quarter profit, which was dragged down by tariffs and weak tractor demand. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires
